title: faramea umbellifera
intro: Faramea umbellifera, also known as the yellow faramea, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Faramea umbellifera include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
